topic: GameSurf NPC Kit — Infrastructure, Training Pipeline & Reliability

context_rules.rule: ALWAYS use host LAN IP (e.g. 192.168.0.x) instead of host.docker.internal for WSL environments.

context_rules.trigger: configuring LM Studio for GPU or encountering Flash Attention disabled errors
context_rules.rule: Always explicitly set 'GPU Offload' to 'Max' (not 'Auto') in LM Studio's Hardware settings. If 'Auto' is used, layers spill to the CPU to avoid OOM, which instantly crashes/disables Flash Attention and tanks performance. Use Q4_K_M quantization for Gemma 4 E4B to fit the 6GB RTX 3060 VRAM.
NEVER add 'custom_image' or 'openai_base_url' keys; they break the CLI 2.x parser. Use docker/start-studio-lmstudio.sh instead.
Inspect docker/supabase-studio-lmstudio/runtime-configure-studio.js to see how env vars are baked into Next.js bundles.
ALWAYS use PM2, not raw bash '&' backgrounding. PM2 provides auto-restart on crash and log rotation. Command: pm2 start scripts/god_memory_worker.py --name god-memory-worker --interpreter python3
The worker has SIGINT/SIGTERM handlers — it will finish its current job before exiting. Use 'pm2 stop god-memory-worker' for graceful shutdown. NEVER kill -9 while the graph rebuild is running.

context_rules.trigger: executing any background scripts or testing local servers
context_rules.rule: AGENT BEHAVIOR RULE: ALWAYS check for existing/zombie processes first using 'pm2 status', 'ps aux | grep <script_name>', or 'lsof -i :<port>'. You MUST explicitly kill non-useful or failed background processes before starting a new one to prevent port conflicts, memory leaks, and silent failures.

ALWAYS use HNSW indexes (not IVFFlat). IVFFlat builds bad clusters on empty tables. HNSW parameters: m=16, ef_construction=64 for 384-dimensional cosine vectors. Example: CREATE INDEX name ON table USING hnsw (embedding vector_cosine_ops) WITH (m = 16, ef_construction = 64);

Use BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5 (384 dimensions) for local NPC memory. Do NOT switch to 768 or 1536 dim models without also running the fix_embedding_vector_dimensions migration. Distance metric MUST be cosine (vector_cosine_ops).

context_rules.trigger: running GPU-heavy ML tasks (Unsloth training, embedding)
context_rules.rule: Keep ML workloads (train_surf_llama.py, god_memory_worker.py) on the WSL host — NOT in Docker. Docker GPU passthrough adds VRAM overhead and latency. Supabase stack stays in Docker; Python ML stack stays native on host.

NotebookLM enforces rate limits: max ~5 queries per burst. The generator batches queries in groups of 5 with a 15s inter-batch cooldown and 4s between individual queries, and 5s between source additions. Do not remove these delays.
Minimum 300 examples per NPC for a 7B LoRA fine-tune. Below 150 causes catastrophic overfitting. The pipeline default is now 300. Use --target-count 500 for best results.

ALL profiles must include multi_turn in their distribution. Standard split: teaching=0.55, quiz=0.25, multi_turn=0.20. Never use a distribution with only 'teaching' and 'quiz' — the model will not learn multi-turn context handling.

Phase 1b (post-generation quality gate) now auto-runs after Phase 1 unless --skip-eval is passed. It samples 30 examples through quality_judge.py. Phase 5 benchmarks now look in benchmarks/npc_dialogue/{npc_key}.json first, then fall back to benchmarks/npc_eval.json.
__pycache__/ and *.pyc are in .gitignore. Never commit them. They are auto-generated per-machine bytecode caches.

dataset_standards.memory_placeholder: {player_memory_summary} must remain as template string in system prompt — never pre-fill it
